cards.back: Prim’s Algorithm is a greedy algorithm used to find the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a graph with no negative weight cycles.
Kruskal’s Algorithm is a greedy algorithm used to find the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) of a graph with no negative weight cycles.
Dijkstra’s Algorithm is a greedy algorithm used to find the shortest path between nodes in a graph with non-negative weights.
A Huffman Tree is a binary tree constructed by combining the two nodes with the lowest frequencies (or weights) in a probability distribution or cost structure.
Huffman Coding is a variable-length prefix code that assigns shorter codes to more frequently occurring symbols in a dataset, achieved through the construction of a Huffman Tree.
